title: sbf-lean: machine-checked Lean 4 formalisation of the universal spectral lower bound (Theorem 1)
description: Lean 4 / Mathlib formal-proof companion to the manuscript 'A universal spectral lower bound on the leave-node-out generalisation error of graph-supervised learning' (R. Fossé and G. Pallares, in preparation). Theorem 1 is machine-checked with zero 'sorry', depending only on the three standard Lean axioms (propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound): the transduction identity (SE_V = SE_T + SE_{Tᶜ}); the Bessel/projection floor, discharged for the genuine orthogonal projection P_S y via Submodule.starProjection_inner_eq_zero; the exact slack-cancellation universal_lower_bound (floor ≤ E_T[L_{Tᶜ}(f̂)]); the forced ratio ρ = N/|Tᶜ| ≥ 1 making the transduction rearrangement an identity rather than an assumption; the witness saturation (two-sided tightness, lower_bound_tight); and the headline form floor = (1 − R²_spec)·Var(y). The deterministic core of Theorem 2 — the ERM oracle inequality L_V(fhat) ≤ floor + 2B and the population-risk sandwich — is also formalised, with the finite-sample value of the deviation bound B (transductive Rademacher / Berry–Esseen concentration) isolated as a hypothesis, beyond current Mathlib.
notes: Formal-proof companion to the structural-bounds-framework submission. Toolchain: Lean v4.31.0, Mathlib pinned in lake-manifest.json (identical pin to od-lean, so the olean cache is shared). Build: lake exe cache get && lake build. The .lake build directory is not included; oleans regenerate from the pinned Mathlib.
